definition: readFile(ref,path): branch blob wins (read-your-writes), else current disk bytes, else NotFound
a file enters the session branch only when an agent writes or edits it; untouched files are never copied in
reads for untouched paths return the working tree's current bytes, enabling edits of any path incl. gitignored
Stop-time write of only touched branch files back to disk; uses readBlobFromTree (no overlay fallback)
the dedicated bare git repo at .git-fs/ (gitignored, publishable); holds pure deltas
env override for the store path; defaults to .git-fs
a unique temp GIT_INDEX_FILE per tree mutation so the store's own index is never disturbed (spec §6)
gitfs/<session-id>; starts empty, accumulates one commit per edit
Session-Id: <id> appended to commit messages on gitfs/* branches; idempotent
optional record in .git-fs/session/tombstones of an untracked disk file deleted via git_fs_rm; materialize unlinks it
git merge-tree --write-tree (git ≥2.38) used for clean merge / conflict detection

mergeLand(target,theirs): atomically land theirs onto a branch via a bounded recompute+CAS loop (update-ref with old-oid); recomputes against the new tip if the ref moved. Safe under concurrent landers vs a hand-rolled update-ref
D1: plain ESM .mjs run by Node ≥20; no transpile/bundle/output dir
D2: no runtime npm deps; shell out to the system git binary
